subject_options: Woodfire Westport — one conversion gap worth fixing
Something small that could change how the site performs
email_body:
I came across Woodfire Westport while researching hospitality businesses in Co. Mayo and wanted to reach out directly.

With 573 Google reviews at 4.6 stars, the reputation you have built is genuinely one of the strongest for any restaurant in the county. What stood out to me is that the current site has no booking system or contact form, so visitors arriving with intent to dine have no direct on-site action to take. That is a gap worth closing given the volume of goodwill already working in your favour.

I work with a web agency based in Ireland and we offer a focused conversion sprint — adding a reservation or enquiry widget and surfacing your review rating on-site — delivered within your existing WordPress setup without disrupting what is already working.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call this week so I can walk you through exactly what these changes would look like and how quickly they could be in place?

Best regards
[Your name]
cta: Would you be open to a 15-minute call this week so I can walk you through exactly what these changes would look like and how quickly they could be in place?
follow_up_body: I wanted to follow up on the email I sent last week about adding a booking or enquiry pathway to the Woodfire Westport site. The core idea was straightforward — a small conversion sprint within your existing WordPress setup, using your 4.6-star reputation as a trust signal on-site. I did not want to assume who handles the website on your end. Is this something you would be open to exploring over the coming weeks, or would it be worth pointing me to the right person?
personalisation_tokens_used: 573 Google reviews at 4.6 stars — used as the anchor proof point for the trust asset framing
No booking system or contact form present on the site — used as the specific conversion gap referenced in the email body
